purpose:
Lightweight zero-dependency Markdown renderer specifically tuned for the subset Claude produces. Used by ClaudeView/MetricsPanel/AgentDetails to render assistant messages safely as innerHTML. Supports fenced code blocks, inline code, bold/italic, H1-H3, ordered/unordered lists, paragraphs (blank-line separated), single-newline→
, horizontal rules, blockquotes, and pipe-delimited tables with alignment. Uses placeholder substitution to protect code blocks from inline processing, then HTML-escapes raw text before applying inline formatting — output is safe for direct innerHTML assignment.
gotchas: The output is HTML-escaped + tagged — assigning to innerHTML is safe BUT only if you trust this exact implementation; do not concatenate with unescaped text afterwards.
Fenced code block regex /```(\w*)\n?([\s\S]*?)```/ is non-greedy — nested or unclosed code fences will cause unexpected output.
Inline code is processed BEFORE bold/italic via placeholder \x00IC\x01{idx}\x00 to avoid `**foo**` being bolded inside backticks.
Bold/italic order matters — bold (**) processed first because the italic regex would match the inner asterisks otherwise.
Blockquote detection looks for ^>\s? (after HTML-escaping) NOT ^>\s? — the input has already been escaped at this stage.
Tables require a separator row matching |---| pattern on the line AFTER the header — without it the line is treated as a paragraph.

Single newline within a paragraph is converted to
via paraLines.join('
') — multi-paragraph text MUST use blank lines.
Horizontal rule '---' must be the entire trimmed line — '--- something' becomes a paragraph.
No support for: links ([text](url)), images, nested lists, task lists, strikethrough, or HTML passthrough — Claude rarely produces these so they were intentionally skipped.
Empty/non-string inputs return '' silently — no error.
patterns: Placeholder + restore pattern (fenced code blocks → \x00CODE_BLOCK_N\x00 → restored at end) — keeps protected content untouched by intermediate transforms.
Block-level processing line-by-line with i counter (manual incrementing) — allows lookahead (table separator) and multi-line consumption.
HTML-escape before applying tags so output is XSS-safe — the inverse order would produce bypasses.
conventions: Public API: single renderMarkdown(text) — keep additional features internal.
